summary: Backend fully functional (9/9 pytest cases passing). Frontend welcome + profile + map screens render and navigation/search/analysis all wire up correctly, BUT the Leaflet map itself does NOT render on the web preview because react-native-webview is unsupported on web. Additionally zero testID attributes exist on interactive elements, making automated UI testing brittle.

ui_bugs.issue: Map screen displays literal text 'React Native WebView does not support this platform.' on web preview. react-native-webview has no web implementation, so Leaflet tiles, markers and route polyline are never rendered in browser. Core feature of the app (visual map) is broken on web.
No testID props on buttons, inputs, toggles, search results, vehicle-type cards, axle chips, save button, end-route button, etc. Automated testing has to rely on fragile text selectors.

After selecting a destination, 'Analyzing route…' indicator appears but the expected bottom sheet with ETA/distance/warnings/weather strip and the 'End Route' button were not visible within ~20s. Search results dropdown also stays open and obscures map area. Could be related to the WebView not loading (no 'mapReady' message arrives so state may remain stuck).

integration_issues.flow: Destination selection → /api/route → /api/route-analysis → map polyline + bottom sheet
integration_issues.issue: Route + analysis APIs succeed on backend (verified via pytest and backend logs), but on web the polyline never draws (WebView broken) and bottom-sheet UI did not surface during test. Needs verification on native (Expo Go) OR a web-compatible map fallback.

issues: Warning banner 'React Native WebView does not support this platform.' is rendered as unstyled red text bleeding over the header on web.
Search results dropdown does not auto-close after selecting a result, covering the map area.

react_native_issues.issue: Library has no web implementation. Must use Platform.OS guard to render an <iframe srcDoc={leafletHtml}/> on web, or switch to react-leaflet for web and keep WebView for iOS/Android.
Console warning: 'props.pointerEvents is deprecated. Use style.pointerEvents' - non-blocking but should migrate.

action_items: Fix map rendering on web: wrap WebView with Platform.OS check; on web render an <iframe> with the same Leaflet HTML (srcDoc or data URL) and bridge postMessage both ways. Alternatively add react-leaflet for web only.

Add testID props to every interactive element: setup-profile-button, skip-to-map-link, vehicle-type-{HGV|LGV|Truck|Van}, name-input, height-input, width-input, length-input, weight-input, axles-{n}, hazmat-toggle, avoid-tolls-toggle, avoid-motorways-toggle, save-profile-button, save-and-drive-button, search-input, search-result-{i}, end-route-button, bottom-sheet, weather-strip, warning-item-{i}.
Ensure search results dropdown collapses after a result is tapped so the bottom sheet and map are visible.
Verify the full route flow end-to-end once map rendering is fixed (select destination → polyline → ETA + warnings + weather strip → End Route clears state).
Investigate deprecated pointerEvents warning (probably inside one of the third-party libs - low priority).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is well structured (~380 lines). No MongoDB _id leakage (proper projection {_id:0} used).
All backend external calls (Nominatim/OSRM/Overpass/Open-Meteo) properly async with httpx, timeouts set, fallback Overpass endpoints - good.
Overpass query result might be empty if endpoints rate-limit; current fallback returns empty elements silently. Consider surfacing a degraded-mode flag to UI.
Frontend map.tsx relies solely on react-native-webview which breaks web - should use Platform-specific rendering.
No testID attributes anywhere - violates testing convention mandated in guidelines.
seed_data_creation: Ephemeral truck profiles keyed by TEST_<uuid> device_ids were created during pytest; they are harmless and can be cleaned up later if needed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend suite at /app/backend/tests/test_haulsafe_api.py covers all 5 endpoints and is green. Re-run after main agent fixes web map rendering and adds testIDs. Then run Playwright flow: open /, tap SET UP TRUCK PROFILE, fill profile, tap SAVE & DRIVE, search 'manchester', select first UK result, wait 20-30s, verify polyline visible + bottom sheet shows distance+duration+warnings, tap End Route to clear.
rca_of_issue: Root cause of web map blank: react-native-webview does not ship a web build - on web it returns a placeholder component ('React Native WebView does not support this platform.'). Because the Leaflet HTML + JS bridge (setRoute, setWarnings, mapReady postMessage) all live inside the WebView, on web none of the map/polyline/markers render and the parent never receives 'mapReady', so any code gated on that event may also stall. Mitigation: render an <iframe srcDoc={html}/> with window.postMessage bridging on Platform.OS==='web' and keep WebView for native.
